response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with solid craftsmanship, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++. His database experience centers on MySQL and MongoDB with no stated SQL Server or Oracle work, and he has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems background — RIDE Capital was retail-investment fintech built on Node/TypeScript, not investment banking. The low-latency systems work and CI/CD strengths are real but do not substitute for the missing mandatory skills.
strengths: Experience building low-latency, high-throughput backend systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions
Some professional C/C++ exposure (National Instruments) plus Java and scripting versatility
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~7 months) and dated, versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain experience
Strong general engineering fundamentals and performance-systems background
